item.title: Tooth & Tail BY ELIZABETH A.M. KEEL
item.description:
Sunday, September 27, 2020 at 6 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

WORLD PREMIERE Tooth & Tail BY ELIZABETH A.M. KEEL Presented by Mildred’s Umbrella Theater Company Jam-packed with princesses, dragons, pirates and more, Tooth & Tail is part fairy-tale, part rescue mission, part love story, and all adventure. Tooth & Tail has been developed and workshopped through

From The DeLUXE Theater Houston

item.title: Classical Theatre Company presents Nevermore
item.description:
Friday, October 8, 2021 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Classical Theatre Company celebrates its thirteenth season – and return to live performances – with a brand-new adaptation of selected stories by the master of Gothic Horror: Edgar Allan Poe. Nevermore: Tales of Edgar Allan Poe will comprise four of Poe’s short stories: The Tell-Tale Heart, William

item.title: A Krush Christmas Auditions
item.description:
Saturday, November 6, 2021 at 4 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The DeLuxe is looking for singers, actors, and dancers to perform ‘A Krush Christmas’ for our upcoming Kids Christmas show. Auditions will be held Saturday, November 6, 2021 from 4:00 – 6:00 p.m. If you have any questions, please call us at (281) 968-0059.

From The DeLUXE Theater Houston

Event details

item.title: Mildred’s Umbrella presents El Huracán
item.description:
Thursday, November 11, 2021 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

El Huracán honors four generations of Cuban-American women who navigate life’s tempests together. Valeria drifts as dementia floods her mind. Ximena sinks as unforgiveness surges inside her. Miranda wades through the torrential mistakes of her past. And Val searches through the rubble to piece toge

From The DeLUXE Theater Houston

item.title: Moore Vision Entertainment presents Grease
item.description:
Friday, November 26, 2021 at 7:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The world’s best-loved musical is back! Grittier and more glamorous than ever before. After a whirlwind summer romance, leather-clad greaser Danny and girl-next-door Sandy are unexpectedly reunited when she transfers to Rydell High for senior year. item.title: Urge presented by Peter McNeil
item.description:
Friday, December 3, 2021 at 6:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

A husband, in denial of an addiction, must choose between conquering the “urge” within or risk losing his family and ultimately his life.

item.title: A Krush Christmas
item.description:
Friday, December 17, 2021 at 7:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The DeLUXE Theater presents the debut performance of the DeLUXE K!DS. A Krush Christmas is holiday production written and directed by 5th Ward native Marquise Elder. The story follows a young girl whose Christmas is crushed when an unexpected guest arrives. Grace Dada is the Musical Director. This

item.description:
Saturday, December 18, 2021 at 11 AM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Join us for a delicious brunch and an incredible holiday performance of A Krush Christmas written and directed by 5th Ward native Marquise Elder. item.title: Classical Theatre Company presents R.U.R.
item.description:
Wednesday, January 12, 2022 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The second of the trio of local professional premieres for the season will be R.U.R. by Karel Čapek. Subtitled Rossum’s Universal Robots, this Czech play which celebrated its 100th anniversary on January 25, 2021. It explores the very nature of life as the characters in the play have created robots

item.title: Who Yo’ People?
item.description:
Sunday, April 3, 2022 at 12:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Who Yo’ People? is a documentary film that explores the African heritage of Louisiana. It takes the audience on a journey through the vibrant history and culture of the peoples who would become known as Louisiana Creoles, while interrogating ideas of identity. The film premiere will be presented in

From The DeLUXE Theater Houston

item.title: Lyons Avenue Renaissance Festival
item.description:
Saturday, April 9, 2022 at 12 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

The Lyons Avenue Renaissance Festival celebrates the history, art, and culture of Houston’s historic 5th Ward. Activities include Houston’s only egg drop by helicopter! Over 50,000 goodie-filled, plastic eggs are airdropped at 3 PM on festival day. Featuring live-music stages with authentic blues,

From The DeLUXE Theater Houston

item.title: The War of the Worlds
item.description:
Wednesday, April 13, 2022 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

Arguably H.G. Wells’ most enduring story, The War of the Worlds, first published in its entirety in 1898, is a science-fiction tale of planetary invasion by creatures from Mars. Famous for his explorations into human nature through the fantastical, Wells’ novel takes aim at the imperialism which wa

item.title: The Mother Project: A Collaboration to Honor Black Mothers and their Children
item.description:
Thursday, May 19, 2022 at 8 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

In collaboration with Esurient Arts, and directed by Dabrina Sandifer, the production was created by a diverse group of six female artists in Mildred’s Umbrella and Esurient Arts, based on direct interviews with 5 black mothers and a doula. The show was created based on interviews from black Americ

item.title: DeLuxe K!ds Performing Arts Workshop
item.description:
Saturday, May 21, 2022 at 10 AM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

item.title: A Crime on the Bayou
item.description:
Thursday, October 13, 2022 at 6:30 PM

Location: 3303 Lyons Ave Houston, TX 77020

A CRIME ON THE BAYOU tells the story of Gary Duncan, a Black teenager arrested and convicted for touching a white boy’s arm in Plaquemines Parish, Louisiana in 1966. A young Jewish attorney, Richard Sobol, leaves his prestigious D.C. firm to volunteer in New Orleans and challenges his unfair arrest.

From The DeLUXE Theater Houston

item.title: Earlybirds Club
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: White Oak Music Hall - Downstairs, 2915 N Main St, Houston, TX

Doors: 6pm Earlybirds Club is a dance party for women and gender expansive folks that starts at 6pm and ends at 10pm ? because we have sh*t to do in the mornin DJ Caribbeats spins the songs that live in your bones: 80s, 90s, and 2000s pop, hip-hop, new wave, and R&B. But Earlybirds Club is more than a night out. It?s a permission slip to take up space. To be loud, weird, wild, sweaty, sparkly, sentimental, and completely yourself. It?s a judgment-free dance floor built on joy, kindness, connection, and the shared understanding that sensible footwear is a love language. And because joy is even better when it gives back, 10% of ticket proceeds go to the Susie Lee Legacy Fund, supporting nonprofits that uplift women, gender-expansive people, and communities who need it most. This show is 21+ All General Admission Tickets are Standing Room only IMPORTANT: WHITE OAK MUSIC HALL IS A CASHLESS VENUE. Please be sure to bring a debit or credit card. item.title: Punk Rock Garage Sale
item.description:
Sunday, July 19, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Bad Astronaut Brewery, 1519 Fulton Street, Houston, TX, 77009, United States

Insomnia Gallery and Deep End Records are excited to announce the date for our July Punk Rock Garage Sale! If you haven’t caught on yet, these happen on the 3rd Sunday of EVERY MONTH at Bad Astronaut brewing! While summer is heating up, stay relatively cool as this event is ***ALL INDOORS!*** and the event space itself now has AC! There will be OVER 50 VENDORS inside this massive adult playground of a venue which features pool tables, foosball, air hockey, cornhole and more (and all FREE to play!) We’ve got makers, artists, collectibles, thrift and vintage, music, books, video games, movies and vendors selling so much more! If you’re looking for a gift or to treat yourself, come to Insomnia Gallery’s Punk Rock Garage Sale for a true chance to score! NEW VENDORS EVERY MONTH! We’ll mix in a few pro vendors and artists as well to bring you a truly unique market each and every month! About this event:Based on the punk rock garage sales that took place regularly at Walter’s Downtown, Insomnia Gallery has been keeping this tradition alive for the last 6 years! DIY Forever! Sale hours: 2:00pm – 6:00pm Location: Bad Astronaut Brewing at 1519 Fulton FREE street parking on Sunday in addition to their back parking lot and grass parking lot! ** INTERESTED IN SETTING UP? Contact deependhtx@gmail.com with a general description of the items you’d like to sell and we’ll get back with you asap! Table fee ranges from $15-$25 depending on setup size!

From Bad Astronaut Brewery

Thursday, July 23, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Bad Astronaut & Photon Air Hockey Present AIR HOCKEY DOUBLES TOURNAMENT Every Thursday | 7:30 – 10 PM | Free Entry Join us every Thursday in the taproom for our exciting Air Hockey Tournaments! item.title: Friko Something Worth Waiting For : The Tour
item.description:
Thursday, September 10, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: White Oak Music Hall - Upstairs , 2915 N Main St,, Houston, TX

Doors: 7pm This show is ALL AGES All General Admission Tickets are Standing Room only IMPORTANT: WHITE OAK MUSIC HALL IS A CASHLESS VENUE. Please be sure to bring a debit or credit card. Friko has partnered with PLUS1 so that $1 from every ticket will go to Santa for a Day, a certified 501(c)(3) Public Charity who answers letters to Santa from kids in underserved communities. This two-pronged literacy component is vital to their mission: Volunteers helping, teaching youngsters the power of the pen? and then donors rewarding their efforts, by translating their wishes into reality, not only positively reinforces their nascent literacy skills, but it also helps participants author a different, more positive narrative about life, one sparked by optimism and the vision of a brighter future. santaforaday.org Doors: 7pm This show is ALL AGES All General Admission Tickets are Standing Room only IMPORTANT: WHITE OAK MUSIC HALL IS A CASHLESS VENUE. Please be sure to bring a debit or credit card. Friko has partnered with PLUS1 so that $1 from every ticket will go to Santa for a Day, a certified 501(c)(3) Public Charity who answers letters to Santa from kids in underserved communities. This two-pronged literacy component is vital to their mission: Volunteers helping, teaching youngsters the power of the pen? and then donors rewarding their efforts, by translating their wishes into reality, not only positively reinforces their nascent literacy skills, but it also helps participants author a different, more positive narrative about life, one sparked by optimism and the vision of a brighter future. santaforaday.org
